subject: Computer Science and Applications
version: Official UGC syllabus applicable from June 2019 onwards; verified against NTA June 2026 bulletin

topics.name: Teaching: concept, objectives and levels
topics.description: Meaning and objectives of teaching; memory, understanding and reflective levels; characteristics and basic requirements.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Teaching: concept, objectives and levels. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Learner characteristics
topics.description: Academic, social, emotional and cognitive characteristics of adolescent and adult learners; individual differences.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Learner characteristics. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Factors affecting teaching
topics.description: Teacher, learner, support material, instructional facilities, learning environment and institution.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Factors affecting teaching. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Methods of teaching
topics.description: Teacher-centred and learner-centred methods; offline and online methods including SWAYAM, SWAYAM Prabha and MOOCs.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Methods of teaching. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Teaching support systems
topics.description: Traditional, modern and ICT-based teaching support systems.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Teaching support systems. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Evaluation systems
topics.description: Elements and types of evaluation, CBCS evaluation, computer-based testing and innovations in evaluation.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Evaluation systems. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Research meaning and approaches
topics.description: Meaning, types and characteristics of research; positivism and post-positivistic approach.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Research meaning and approaches. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Methods of research
topics.description: Experimental, descriptive, historical, qualitative and quantitative methods.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Methods of research. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Steps of research
topics.description: Problem identification, literature review, objectives, design, data, analysis, interpretation and reporting.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Steps of research. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Thesis and article writing
topics.description: Academic structure, format and referencing styles.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Thesis and article writing. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: ICT in research
topics.description: Digital discovery, data collection, analysis, collaboration and dissemination.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for ICT in research. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Research ethics
topics.description: Integrity, consent, attribution, conflicts, fabrication, falsification and plagiarism.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Research ethics. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Reading comprehension
topics.description: Read a passage, identify claims, inference, tone, structure, assumptions and evidence, and answer only from the passage.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Reading comprehension. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Mathematical Logic
topics.description: Propositional and predicate logic, equivalences, normal forms, predicates, quantifiers, nested quantifiers and rules of inference.

topics.name: Sets and Relations
topics.description: Set operations, representation and properties of relations, equivalence relations and partial ordering.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Sets and Relations. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Counting, Induction and Discrete Probability
topics.description: Counting, pigeonhole principle, permutations, combinations, inclusion–exclusion, mathematical induction, probability and Bayes theorem.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Counting, Induction and Discrete Probability. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Group Theory
topics.description: Groups, subgroups, semigroups, products and quotients, isomorphism, homomorphism, automorphism, rings, integral domains, fields and applications.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Group Theory. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Graph Theory
topics.description: Simple, multi and weighted graphs; paths, circuits, shortest paths, Eulerian and Hamiltonian structures, planar graphs, colouring, bipartite graphs, trees, codes, traversals, spanning trees and cut sets.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Graph Theory. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Boolean Algebra
topics.description: Boolean functions, representation and simplification.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Boolean Algebra. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Optimization
topics.description: Linear programming, graphical and simplex methods, sensitivity, integer programming, transportation, assignment, PERT–CPM, resource levelling and cost considerations.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Optimization. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Digital Logic Circuits and Components
topics.description: Digital computers, gates, Boolean algebra, map simplification, combinational and sequential circuits, flip-flops, ICs, decoders, multiplexers, registers, counters and memory.
topics.offlineNote: Focus on the definition, distinguishing features, standard examples and common exam traps for Digital Logic Circuits and Components. After reading the official scope, practise recall first and then timed MCQs.

topics.name: Data Representation and Arithmetic

topics.name: Register Transfer and Microoperations
